message:
fix(atc): add recency cutoff to cold-start hydration, gate writes by executor mode

Two fixes for ATC operator loop performance:

1. atc_sync_population_from_db now skips agents whose last_active_ts is
   older than a configurable recency window (default 7 days, tunable via
   AM_ATC_POPULATION_RECENCY_SECS). Agents silent beyond this window are
   already Dead from the liveness evaluator's perspective — hydrating them
   generates an O(all-historical-agents) burst of tick effects on every
   cold start, saturating the pending_effects queue and pegging CPU at
   ~99% indefinitely on mailboxes with large agent populations.

2. Durable experience row writes (append_atc_experience_for_effect) are
   now gated to Live and Canary executor modes only. Shadow and DryRun
   modes suppress all real actions, so recording experiences for them
   produces write churn (~46.5 rows/sec) without any learning value.

Closes #90

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
